In 4Q25, C1 cash cost also totaled US$ 21.3/t, 13% higher y/y, in line with guidance. Iron ore all-in costs reached US$ 54.2/t, 3% lower y/y in 2025 and US$ 54.3/t, 10% higher y/y in 4Q25. Copper all-in costs were US$ -881/t in the quarter, while nickel all-in costs declined 35% y/y to US$ 9,001/t, mainly driven by strong by-product revenues and operational improvements across both segments.
